page: Microsoft Azure FX32-8mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family. It is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 4 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores, running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for tile and int8 operations. The system is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high resource density of 84.0 GB of RAM per core.

Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The server includes 1890 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no hardware GPU accelerators present on this instance type.

In benchmark testing, the FX32-8mds_v2 demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, achieving a Geekbench single-core score of 2352.0 and a text processing score of 2945.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are also strong, with gzip compression reaching 34.73 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.39 GB/sec. However, multi-core and multi-threaded benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core at 8876.24 and Geekbench multi-core at 8736.0, fall into the average range. Large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes are measured at 71.78 GB/sec and 52.07 GB/sec, respectively.

The primary tradeoff of this configuration is the massive memory capacity paired with a relatively low core count, which limits parallel processing scaling. This is evident in the weak performance for medium-sized 7B LLM text generation, which averages 7.72 tokens/sec. Consequently, this server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling, such as large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure FX32-8mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features an exceptionally high memory capacity of 672.0 GB, providing 84.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 1890 GB of local storage. Virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V, the server lacks GPU accelerators. In benchmark evaluations, it delivers top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2352.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression of 34.73 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core performance and medium LLM text generation speeds are average to weak due to the 8-vCPU limit. This resource profile makes the server appropriate for memory-intensive, low-vCPU workloads such as in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and enterprise applications requiring massive memory density per core without requiring extensive parallel processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX32-8mds_v2 is an 8-vCPU Intel-based server featuring 672 GB of RAM, 1890 GB of local storage, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX32-8mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, virtualized on Hyper-V.
tagline: An ultra-high memory density server featuring 672 GB RAM and strong single-core performance for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM, providing 84.0 GB of memory per core
Includes 1890 GB of local storage with premium storage capability
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing benchmarks
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-intensive workloads
